items.title: Thailand presses on with Cambodia border fence after 2025 clashes
items.summary: PONG NAM RON: Thailand has completed the first section of a permanent border fence along parts of its frontier with Cambodia, the Thai military said on Wednesday, as tensions persist between the two Southeast Asian neighbours following two rounds of fighting last year. Chief of Defence Forces General Ukrit Boontanon described the 1.3-km stretch of reinforced concrete panels topped with steel mesh and concertina wire in the eastern Pong Nam Ron district as "stable, strong and durable", saying it had been designed for long-term security. Ukrit said the military planned to build more than 7 km of additional fencing connected to the existing section, while assessing other areas of the 817-km frontier where terrain and environmental conditions would allow similar construction.

items.title: Anwar says Malaysia will maintain ban on Israeli citizens entering country
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said Malaysia would maintain its policy of barring Israeli citizens from entering the country despite criticism from eight members of the United States Congress. He said Malaysia, as an independent and sovereign nation, had long upheld the policy, stressing that it was not a new decision but one that had been in place for decades. "Several members of the US Congress have issued statements criticising me over Malaysia's decision to bar Israeli citizens from entering the country, including from participating in companies and other activities.

items.title: Mynews acquires RM24.7mil Rawang industrial land for new distribution centre
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: MyNews Holdings Bhd's wholly owned subsidiary Mynews Retail Sdn Bhd has entered into a sale and purchase agreement (SPA) with Thung Hing Metal Industry Sdn Bhd to acquire 13.5 acres leasehold industrial land in Rawang, Selangor for RM24.70 million. "Securing the land now lets the company move ahead with construction, address capacity constraints and avoid rising land costs or the shrinking availability of suitable sites, with the new distribution centre expected to enhance supply chain efficiency, strengthen the company's asset base, and support continued business expansion over the medium to long term," it said. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Woman dies after losing contact with group during Pulau Tioman dive
items.summary: KUANTAN, July 22 — A local woman died while diving in the waters off Kampung Salang, Pulau Tioman, last Sunday. Rompin police chief Supt Sharif Shai Sharif Mondoi said the victim, Heng Jing Ying, 31, had taken part in the ‘Aware Coral’ activity organised by Tioman Dive Buddy Centre, Kampung Genting, from July 17 to 18. He said on July 19, the victim, together with nine other participants, an instructor and a dive master, joined a fun dive at Seafan, Batu Malang, Pulau Tulai.

items.title: IGB Reit Q2 net profit rises to RM139.27mil on additional income, profit contribution
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: IGB Real Estate Investment Trust's (IGB REIT) net profit for the second quarter ended June 30, 2026 (Q2 2026) rose 50.6 per cent to RM139.27 million against RM92.50 million in the same quarter a year ago. Revenue jumped to RM240.95 million, a 50.5 per cent increase from RM160.09 million in Q2 2025, while net property income was RM181.2 million, an increase of 51.1 per cent compared with the preceding year quarter's RM119.9 million, the company said in a filing with Bursa Malaysia today. For the first half of 2026 (1H 2026), IGB REIT's net profit surged to RM302.08 million against RM199.08 million in 1H 2025, while revenue widened to RM502.29 million from RM331.53 million previously.

items.title: Investigators search Deutsche Bank's Frankfurt headquarters
items.summary: FRANKFURT: German tax investigators were searching offices at Deutsche Bank's headquarters in Frankfurt on Wednesday as they investigate alleged tax fraud transactions, Die Welt and other media reported. "Deutsche Bank is being searched as a third party in this matter and we are cooperating fully," Deutsche Bank said, declining to comment further. The probe relates to alleged tax fraud transactions known as cum-cum, according to media reports.

items.title: Kidnap victim rescued in RM7m ransom case detained over alleged drug trafficking links
items.summary: SUBANG JAYA, July 22 — A kidnap victim rescued within 48 hours after police foiled a RM7 million ransom plot has been detained over alleged involvement in syndicated drug trafficking activities, federal police said today. Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department director Commissioner Datuk M. Kumar said the victim was detained under the Dangerous Drugs (Special Preventive Measures) Act after the Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department uncovered alleged links to drug-related activities on July 21. Apart from the victim, Kumar said two other suspects were also arrested, bringing the total number of individuals detained in connection with the case to eight.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia ends lower amid profit-taking, higher oil prices
items.summary: Turnover narrowed to 3.43 billion units valued at RM2.50 billion from 3.48 billion units valued at RM2.79 billion on Tuesday. Among active stocks, Aimax and Nextgram Holdings inched up half a sen each to 1.5 sen and 4.5 sen, respectively, and Aimflex added three sen to 12 sen. Zetrix AI eased 1.5 sen to 71.5 sen, and Tanco Holdings decreased one sen to 30.5 sen. Warrants turnover dwindled to 970.20 million units worth RM130.98 million versus 1.11 billion units worth RM138.80 million previously.

items.title: Sabah Native Courts Bill strengthens legal framework for customary justice
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The newly passed Native Courts Bill provides a more comprehensive, modern and structured legal framework to strengthen the Native Court institution. "After more than three decades, comprehensive reform is necessary to ensure that the customary justice system meets present-day needs without compromising the roots, identity and unique customs of Sabah's Native communities. Mohd Arifin, who is also the Local Government and Housing Minister, said the Bill streamlines the hierarchy of the Native Courts through the establishment of the Native District Court, Native High Court and Native Court of Appeal, providing clearer definitions of each court level.

items.title: N9 polls: Candidate appeal key to razor-thin Juasseh contest
items.summary: The three-cornered contest involving BN, PH and Bersatu has prompted both major contenders to argue that their respective strengths will resonate with voters. BN candidate Datuk Ismail Lasim believes Bersatu's decision to contest under its own logo, instead of the PN symbol, could work in BN's favour. He said voters were more familiar with the PN logo than Bersatu's party symbol, potentially giving BN a slight advantage.

items.title: 108 undocumented migrants detained in Johor immigration raid
items.summary: They were arrested in a joint operation by the Johor Immigration Department and the Malaysia Border Control and Protection Agency. Johor Immigration director Datuk Mohd Rusdi Mohd Darus said the operation was aimed at strengthening enforcement of immigration laws and curbing the abuse of visit passes, work passes and immigration facilities. Rusdi said all those detained were suspected of committing offences under the Immigration Act 1959/63 and the Immigration Regulations 1963.

items.title: Bank Negara's international reserves at US$131.8bil as at July 15
items.summary: Bank Negara reported that the gross international reserves included foreign currency reserves (US$116.4 billion), the International Monetary Fund (IMF) reserve position (US$1.3 billion), special drawing rights (SDRs) (US$6.0 billion), gold (US$5.8 billion), and other reserve assets (US$2.3 billion). Total assets amounted to RM633.97 billion, comprising mainly gold, foreign financial assets and other reserves including SDRs (RM533.93 billion), Malaysian government papers (RM12.68 billion), deposits with financial institutions (RM2.99 billion), loans and advances (RM20.34 billion), property and equipment (RM4.55 billion) and other assets (RM59.49 billion). Capital and liabilities totalled RM633.97 billion, comprising capital (RM100 million), reserves (RM197.62 billion), currency in circulation (RM183.24 billion), deposits by financial institutions (RM113.06 billion), federal government deposits (RM16.25 billion), other deposits (RM85.63 billion), Bank Negara papers (RM7.75 billion), allocation of SDRs (RM26.71 billion), and other liabilities (RM3.61 billion).

items.title: Anwar urges Negri voters to keep state aligned with Putrajaya
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Pakatan Harapan chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today urged Negri Sembilan voters to continue giving the coalition's mandate to govern the state, saying political stability was essential to ensure continued development and safeguard the people's welfare. He said close cooperation between the state and federal governments would enable development initiatives to continue benefiting the people. "We are developing the state while looking after the people.

items.title: 'We sat down for lunch': Fahmi dismisses talk of Cabinet rift
items.summary: KUALA PILAH: The Cabinet remains united despite recent public exchanges over ministerial discipline during the Negri Sembilan election campaign, Communications Minister Datuk Seri Fahmi Fadzil said today. Responding to Barisan Nasional chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi's remarks that Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim could dismiss BN ministers if dissatisfied with their performance, Fahmi said Anwar's recent warning was simply a reminder for members of the administration to uphold discipline and professionalism. Fahmi said today's Cabinet meeting proceeded smoothly, with ministers continuing to demonstrate good working relations.

items.title: US-Iran conflict flares up again but Malaysian businesses stay prepared with measures in place
items.summary: For many Malaysian businesses, however, the latest developments merely reinforce the need to remain vigilant rather than return to business as usual. Manufacturers, retailers and small businesses continue to operate in an environment shaped by volatile shipping costs, fluctuating energy prices and fragile supply chains that have persisted after geopolitical tensions first emerged. Businesses recognise that global logistics networks and supply chains will take much longer to recover even if tensions ease.

items.title: Police hunt two men over violent robbery of elderly woman in Lenggong
items.summary: District police chief Superintendent Abdul Samad Othman said the victim in her 80s, who lives alone, was attacked after the suspects broke into her house at 5.10am. "The suspects fled with several of the victim's belongings after the robbery. Abdul Samad said police have launched a manhunt for the suspects and are pursuing several leads to identify and locate them.

items.title: Malaysia, US discuss trade ties amid tariff uncertainty
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia and the United States have discussed ways to strengthen bilateral trade ties, including ongoing Sections 301 and 232 investigations that could affect Malaysian exports. Investment, Trade and Industry Minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani said Malaysia hopes any trade measures introduced by the US would take into account the shared interests of both countries. Johari said he raised the matter during his maiden bilateral meeting with US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer.

items.title: Sabah Assembly passes sweeping Native Court reforms, introduces new three-tier system
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 22 — Sabah today passed Bills for sweeping reforms to its traditional Native Court system introducing a new legal framework aimed at modernising customary justice through clearer court structures, merit-based judicial appointments, appeals, legal representation and even remote hearings. What is being reformed is the institution and administration of customary justice so that customs can continue to be preserved and implemented through a more orderly, fair and credible court system.” Among the most significant changes is the restructuring of Sabah’s Native Court hierarchy into three tiers — the District Native Court, the High Native Court and the Native Court of Appeal — providing clearer judicial responsibilities and a more structured appeals process. The Bill also clearly defines the court’s jurisdiction by setting out which matters fall under the Native Courts while reaffirming that civil and Syariah court jurisdictions remain unaffected.

items.title: Anwar posts pantun stressing unity and consensus amid state election campaign
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today shared a message on unity and consensus, posting a four-line Malay pantun underscoring the value of friendship and solidarity. In a post on his official Facebook page, Anwar wrote: "Burung merbah terbang sekawan, Guruh berdentum membelah awan, Berbeza langkah, jangan putus kawan, Selagi muafakat, kita sehaluan." Anwar also shared photographs from a Cabinet meeting attended by, among others, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi and Transport Minister Anthony Loke, all appearing relaxed and in good spirits.

items.title: Ousted Bangladesh PM Hasina vows to return despite death sentence
items.summary: "I will return to Bangladesh. I will appear before the court," said Hasina, 78, in a recent written interview with Kyodo News, without identifying a specific court. Bangladesh has requested India to extradite Hasina.

items.title: Police bust RM27m-a-day World Cup gambling syndicate in Klang Valley
items.summary: SUBANG JAYA, July 22 — A major online betting syndicate with an estimated RM27 million in daily betting transactions has been dismantled by federal police following a series of coordinated operations targeting online gambling activities during the 2026 Fifa World Cup recently. Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department director Commissioner Datuk M Kumar said a series of raids were conducted following investigations by the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) to identify the owners of websites promoting and advertising gambling activities on social media platforms. Police also seized RM199,006.19 in cash and identified betting transactions worth RM35.15 million linked to the illegal activities.

items.title: Court upholds RM5.62mil negligence award in ex-Prolintas CEO medical negligence case
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Court of Appeal recently affirmed the High Court's ruling that a private hospital and two doctors were liable for medical negligence that left former CEO of highway operator, Prolintas Datuk Zainudin A. Kadir with permanent brain damage following routine sinus surgery. The additional award comprised RM504,527.69 in hospital and medical expenses and RM16,960 in legal costs incurred to obtain a court order appointing a committee to manage Zainudin's affairs after he suffered permanent brain damage. According to the case facts, Zainudin was admitted to the hospital on Feb 22, 2016, and underwent nasal surgery four days later.

items.title: Penang police seize RM470k worth of ecstasy, arrest man
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Penang police seized 3.1kg of MDMA worth about RM470,000 in a raid at Teluk Air Tawar on Sunday. State police chief Datuk Dennis Lim said the raid was carried out by the state Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department at a bungalow in Teluk Air Tawar, Butterworth. Lim urged members of the public to continue providing information to police on drug trafficking and other criminal activities.

items.title: Bangladeshi man charged with possessing 11 passports in Kulim
items.summary: KULIM, July 22 — A Bangladeshi man pleaded not guilty at the Magistrate’s Court here today to a charge of possessing 11 Bangladeshi passports without lawful authority last month. Salman Mallik, 49, was charged with possessing 11 passports issued to Bangladeshi citizens for the use of others without lawful authority at a premises in Taman Senangin here, at around 7 pm on June 27. Deputy Public Prosecutor S. Preeyaa offered bail at RM7,000 with two Malaysian guarantors.

items.title: RM27mil-a-day World Cup betting syndicate busted, 161 held
items.summary: SUBANG JAYA: Police have crippled an online gambling syndicate operating under the guise of legitimate businesses, including a computer company, after raids on 19 premises across the Klang Valley led to 161 arrests. Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk M. Kumar said the suspects, detained during Op Soga XI last Thursday, comprised 106 men and 55 women, including six foreigners, aged between 19 and 62. "Police estimate that the syndicate handled betting credit transactions worth about RM27 million daily.

items.title: Motorcyclist killed after being hit from behind at Puncak Alam junction, foreign driver held
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 22 — A foreign national was detained after the car he was driving rammed into a motorcycle that had stopped at a red traffic light along Jalan Persiaran Mokhtar Dahari, Puncak Alam, killing the motorcyclist last night. Sungai Buloh Police chief Supt Mohd Hafiz Muhammad Nor said the 28-year-old was arrested at the scene after police received a report on the crash at 8.23 pm. “When he arrived at the traffic light junction, a car driven by a foreign man struck the rear of the motorcycle, which was stationary at the red traffic light,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Senegal's 'Pink Lake' recovers its rosy hue, reviving salt trade
items.summary: STANDING waist-deep in the waters of Senegal's famed Pink Lake, Seydou Toure uses a shovel to scrape salt from the lakebed that he will later sell to tourists and traders. Overnight, salt harvesters like Toure lost their jobs and tourism slowed to a trickle, devastating the local economy. Roughly 3,000 salt harvesters normally work at the lake located 30 km northeast of Dakar, according to Ibrahima Mbaye, president of a local NGO dedicated to protecting the lake.

items.title: Johor strengthens state administration with 10 new deputy executive committee leaders
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI, July 22 — Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi today announced the appointment of 10 deputy chairmen of the State Executive Council committees to strengthen the state administration and improve service delivery. Onn Hafiz said the move was in line with various strategic initiatives being rolled out to keep Johor on track to become a developed state by 2030 under the ‘Maju Johor 2030’ framework and the Johor Economic Transformation Plan (JETP). Describing the appointments as a significant responsibility and a matter of trust, Onn Hafiz said the deputy committee chairmen were expected to help strengthen government service delivery, expedite the resolution of issues affecting the people, and ensure that state initiatives were implemented effectively.

items.title: Global expertise welcome, but port leaders must understand local challenges, say experts
items.summary: Maritime Network Sdn Bhd chief executive officer (CEO) Datuk Seri Jeyenderan Ramasamy said international appointments were common in the highly specialised maritime industry but should be balanced with stronger local representation on port boards, clearly defined authority over strategic assets and regular regulatory oversight. He said while global experience brought expertise in terminal automation, digitalisation and port expansion, Malaysia should first determine whether qualified local professionals were available. He said Malaysia should remain open to global expertise, provided appointments strengthened governance, transparency, national resilience and the development of future Malaysian port leaders.

items.title: Career diplomat Joshua Harris nominated as next US ambassador to Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The United States has nominated career diplomat Joshua Harris to serve as the next Ambassador to Malaysia. Harris is a career member of the Senior Foreign Service and currently holds the rank of Minister-Counselor. Online checks reveal that Harris is a seasoned diplomat whose career spans several decades of high-level leadership and extensive international representation.

items.title: Johor's first deputy excos hit the ground running
items.summary: Speaking after the announcement of the state's first deputy exco line-up at Kota Iskandar, the newly appointed deputy excos said they were ready to support the Johor government in accelerating policy implementation while ensuring development reached communities across the state. Deputy Exco for Investment, Trade, Consumer Affairs and Human Resources Syed Hussein Syed Abdullah, 42, described his appointment as a major responsibility that would require close cooperation to realise the Maju Johor 2030 agenda. Deputy Exco for Women, Family and Community Development Chan San San, 46, said her experience dealing with social issues would enable her to contribute meaningfully in her new role.

items.title: Japan cities 'cruelly hot' for first time
items.summary: TOKYO: Japanese cities are experiencing "cruelly hot days", a new meteorological term devised this year to warn people of intense heat above 40C, with heatstroke alerts issued across almost the whole country. Three central cities on Tuesday logged scorching heat above the threshold, called "kokushobi" which translates to "cruelly hot day", according to the Japan Meteorological Agency. The agency has issued heatstroke alerts in 41 out of Japan's 47 prefectures, urging the public – especially the many elderly – to take steps to stay cool.

items.title: Police seek William to assist Penang phone scam probe
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Police are seeking a local man known as William to assist investigations into a phone scam syndicate uncovered in Bukit Mertajam recently. State police chief Datuk Dennis Lim said William, 49, whose last known address is in Kluang, Johor, is being sought only as a witness. Investigations into the phone scam syndicate are ongoing.

items.title: Myanmar nationals make up majority of 22,166 immigration detainees
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A total of 22,166 undocumented migrants have been detained at 20 immigration depots nationwide, with more than half of them being Myanmar nationals, as of June 30. Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said 11,194 detainees were from Myanmar, followed by 5,200 Filipinos, 3,247 Indonesians and 1,133 Bangladeshis. Saifuddin said one detainee had been held for 11 years, while eight had been detained for between six and 10 years, followed by 5,035 detainees who had been held for one to five years.

items.title: Lebanon PM says Beirut working for 'complete Israeli withdrawal'
items.summary: ZAWTAR AL-GHARBIYAH: Beirut is working towards a "complete Israeli withdrawal" from areas it occupies, Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam said on Wednesday on a visit to a village where Lebanon's army recently deployed. The Lebanese military had begun deploying in the village on Tuesday. Israeli forces were previously present on its outskirts.

items.title: Rebranded MAHA Pahang begins in Raub tomorrow, targets RM1.3mil sales
items.summary: BERA: The rebranded Pahang Malaysia Agriculture, Horticulture and Agrotourism Exhibition (MAHA) will begin in Raub tomorrow, featuring about 100 entrepreneurs and targeting RM1.3 million in sales. Earlier, Wan Rosdy flagged off 25 vehicles participating in the MAHA Pahang convoy, representing various agencies and departments, including the State Secretary's Office, Pahang Agriculture Department, Veterinary Services Department, Fisheries Department, Federal Agricultural Marketing Authority and Pahang Agricultural Development Corporation, at the Bera District and Land Office. Themed "Agro Lestari Bina Negeri", MAHA Pahang will be held at Dataran Raub, the Raub Silver Jubilee Hall and Taman Tasik Raub from July 23 to July 26.

items.title: Seoul Mayor Oh Se-hoon faces risk of losing office after political funds conviction
items.summary: SEOUL: A South Korean court on Wednesday convicted Seoul Mayor Oh Se-hoon and fined him 10 million won (US$6,759) over a political funds case linked to opinion polls conducted ahead of the 2021 Seoul mayoral by-election. The court found Oh received polling services worth 21 million won and had a longtime supporter cover the costs, in violation of the Political Funds Act, and ordered the forfeiture of 21 million won, according to a court statement. Oh said he could not accept the ruling and would appeal.

items.title: Suspended cop charged with sexually assaulting 11-year-old daughter while on bail
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A suspended police corporal claimed trial at the Ampang Sessions Court today to two charges of physically sexually assaulting his 11-year-old daughter in May. The court heard that the alleged offences were committed while the accused was out on bail for a separate alleged rape case involving his 18-year-old eldest daughter. The court was also informed that the accused is currently on psychiatric medication.

items.title: Prison Department says police probing Suaram’s death in custody, abuse claims
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 22 — The Malaysian Prison Department has confirmed that allegations raised by rights group Suara Rakyat Malaysia (Suaram) involving a death in custody and claims of mistreatment at two prisons are now being investigated by the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM). In a media statement, the department said it was aware of Suaram’s allegations concerning the death of a detainee at one prison institution and separate claims of improper treatment of another inmate at another facility “The Malaysian Prison Department is providing full cooperation to the police in assisting the investigation to ensure that all facts can be obtained comprehensively, transparently, and in accordance with the law,” it said. The statement follows a press release issued by Suaram on July 17 detailing two cases.

items.title: More tremors, another inspection: Inti building declared safe
items.summary: SUBANG JAYA: Another building was declared safe after members of the public reported feeling tremors following the earthquake in northern Sumatra today. The Fire and Rescue Department inspected a building at Inti International College Subang after receiving a report at 12.40pm. A team arrived three minutes later to assess the building for possible cracks or structural damage.

items.title: Personal Data Protection Department investigates Maxis over Khairul Aming data leak
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 22 — The Personal Data Protection Department (JPDP) has launched a probe into the unauthorised disclosure of Maxis customers’ account and billing details after the information surfaced on social media. “JPDP is conducting an investigation under the Personal Data Protection Principles and Section 130 of the Personal Data Protection Act 2010 in relation to the unlawful collection or disclosure of personal data,” it said. The investigation was sparked by a controversy involving beauty entrepreneur and social media personality Khairul Aming, whose private account and billing details were leaked across social media.

items.title: Granddad’s car used as drug store: 16-year-old among eight arrested in JB raids
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 22 — Police have dismantled a synthetic drug ring after arresting eight individuals, including a 16-year-old teenager, in a series of three raids across the city. The operations, conducted on July 18, netted 50 plastic packets of suspected methamphetamine weighing 60.78kg, with a street value estimated at RM3.04 million. Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad confirmed that the arrests were the result of intelligence gathered by the Johor Bahru South district police’s Narcotics Criminal Investigation Division (NCID).

items.title: PDRM prepares security operations for Negeri Sembilan state election
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 22 — The Royal Malaysia Police’s (PDRM) Department of Internal Security and Public Order (KDNKA) has mobilised 200 officers and personnel to support security operations for the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election. “For the Johor state election, KDNKA deployed 850 officers and personnel. For the Negeri Sembilan state election, we expect to deploy about 200 officers and personnel because, compared with Johor, Negeri Sembilan has a smaller number of state constituencies,” he said.

items.title: Defence weighs AGC appeal for driver in fatal Klang crash
items.summary: KLANG, July 22 — The defence is exploring a formal representation to the Attorney-General’s Chambers (AGC) for a technician accused of driving under the influence and causing a fatal crash that killed a delivery rider. The move comes as the 28-year-old accused, R. Saktygaanapathy, pleaded not guilty to a charge of self-administering tetrahydrocannabinol (THC) at the Klang Selatan district police headquarters on March 29. While the drug charge is being contested, Saktygaanapathy faces a far more severe legal battle.

items.title: Amirudin: Pakatan banks on micro-campaign strategy to win over Negeri Sembilan voters
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 22 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) is focusing on a locality-based micro-campaign strategy to deliver the coalition’s message and election pledges directly to grassroots voters in the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election. “In Negeri Sembilan, PH is leading the state government... the stability demonstrated during its first term will be a key selling point. In the 2023 Negeri Sembilan state election, PH won 17 seats, while BN secured 14 and PN five.

items.title: Court of Appeal rules children born out of wedlock cannot inherit Malaysian father’s citizenship
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 22 — The Court of Appeal today unanimously decided that two persons who were born in Malaysia are not entitled to be Malaysian citizens under the Federal Constitution, as their biological Malaysian fathers were not married to their non-Malaysian mothers when they were born. Here’s a quick recap of the facts in their cases based on court documents, and what the Court of Appeal decided today: The Court of Appeal said Malaysia-born children have to follow their mother's citizenship status, if the child is illegitimate or born when the parents were not married. — File picture by Devan Manuel The case of J: Birth certificate once recorded her as Malaysian, had a Malaysian passport J, who will be turning 30 this year, has a DNA test to show her biological ties to her Malaysian father.

items.title: Anwar hails federal-state cooperation in driving Sarawak’s growth and development
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 22 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the close cooperation and shared commitment between the Federal Government and the Sarawak Government continue to strengthen the state’s position as a leader in growth, innovation and inclusive development. In a Facebook post marking Sarawak Day today, Anwar said every initiative undertaken reflects a shared determination to uplift the people, strengthen the economy and ensure lasting prosperity. “Happy Sarawak Day to all Sarawakians,” he said.

items.title: Did you feel it? MetMalaysia says 5.7 magnitude Sumatra quake rattles west coast of peninsula
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 22 — A moderate earthquake in North Sumatra sent tremors rippling across the west coast of Peninsular Malaysia on Tuesday. The Malaysian Meteorological Department reported that the quake struck at 12:18 pm today, registering a magnitude of 5.7. The Meteorological Department is now urging anyone who experienced tremors to provide their feedback via an official survey form .

items.title: Higher oil prices may push petrol, diesel subsidies to RM3.5b monthly, says Liew Chin Tong
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 22 — The government estimates that the RON95 petrol subsidy will reach approximately RM2 billion per month, while the diesel subsidy will amount to about RM1.5 billion per month, if crude oil prices remain at around US$90 per barrel based on current projections. Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong said that, so far, the country’s fiscal position remains strong and fiscal projections are unchanged despite the increase in crude oil prices. “The government also earns revenue from petroleum.

items.title: Negeri Sembilan polls: Sri Tanjung independent candidate to appear in court over social media post
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 22 — Independent candidate contesting the Sri Tanjung seat in the Negeri Sembilan state election, Islah Wahyudi Zainudin, will have to appear before the Sepang Sessions Court on Aug 11 over a charge linked to a social media post. The 46-year-old candidate said he received a notice of the charge from an officer of the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) yesterday. “Yes, an MCMC officer came to my house around midday yesterday.

items.title: Police keep election campaign activities in check as Negeri Sembilan polls heat up
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 22 — The authorities approved 173 out of 178 applications to hold talks and political campaign activities for the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election between July 18 and yesterday. Police chief Datuk Alzafny Ahmad said all approved programmes and political activities are being closely monitored by the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) to ensure they stay within legal bounds and do not jeopardise public safety or order. Over the same period, he said, nine police reports were received, and five investigation papers opened, one of which, opened yesterday, involves an election offence.

items.title: Keropok seller charged over drone tobacco smuggling attempt at Sungai Udang Prison
items.summary: The accused, Mohd Nizam Ab Manaf, 46, made the plea after the charges were read out before Magistrate Sharda Shienha Mohd Suleiman. For the first charge, Mohd Nizam is accused of committing criminal trespass with intent to smuggle prohibited items, namely tobacco, into Sungai Udang Prison using a drone. For the second charge, Mohd Nizam is accused of smuggling tobacco into Sungai Udang Prison using a drone at the same place, time and date.

items.title: Anwar’s Negeri Sembilan campaign debut aims to boost Pakatan election momentum
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 22 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim will hit the campaign trail in Negeri Sembilan today in an effort to further strengthen Pakatan Harapan’s (PH) momentum in the ongoing state election. In the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election, PH is contesting all 36 state seats, with 16 candidates from PKR, 11 from DAP and nine from Amanah. The Election Commission (EC) has set early voting for the Negeri Sembilan state election on July 28, while polling day is on Aug 1.

items.title: Tengku Zafrul celebrates daughter’s graduation in UK, hopes for her homecoming
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 22 — Former minister Datuk Seri Tengku Zafrul Aziz has couched his personal hopes in a celebration of his daughter’s academic achievement, urging her to return to Malaysia once she completes her studies. He made the reminder as he celebrated his daughter Zahra’s graduation from the University of Warwick. Zahra has distinguished herself academically, earning First Class Honours in English Literature.

items.title: Last mile of reform: Samenta calls for local govt overhaul to sustain Malaysia’s competitiveness gain
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 22 — While Malaysia has surged to a historic 15th in the IMD World Competitiveness Ranking, the Small and Medium Enterprises Association of Malaysia (Samenta) warns that these macro-level gains must now trickle down to the SME sector. To achieve this, the association urged the government to target the next critical layer of bureaucracy: local councils (PBTs) and statutory bodies. “SMEs consistently report that unpredictable processing timelines for licenses, permits and renewals as well as erratic enforcement remain a primary driver of rising operational costs and business uncertainties,” Samenta national president Datuk William Ng said in a statement.

items.title: MIC to hold nationwide Fun Run across 137 divisions for 80th anniversary celebrations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 22 — MIC will hold a nationwide Fun Run this Sunday (July 26), with events rolling out simultaneously across all 137 of its divisions as part of the party’s 80th anniversary celebrations, said vice-president Datuk T. Murugiah. The main event, a 5km run, will be held at Taman Tasik Titiwangsa here, with MIC president Tan Sri S.A. Vigneswaran flagging off participants at 7 am. “We are organising the Fun Run not only to celebrate MIC’s 80th anniversary but also to foster unity among Malaysians of all races.

items.title: Tawau man who forced daughter to book rooms where he raped her gets 105 years’ prison
items.summary: TAWAU, July 21 — A 53-year-old man was sentenced to 105 years in prison and 70 strokes of the cane by the Tawau Sessions Court after pleading guilty to seven counts of raping his eldest daughter, in a case prosecutors described as premeditated and aggravated by emotional manipulation. Judge Ahmad Faizadh Yahaya handed down the sentence immediately after the man pleaded guilty to all charges read against him. According to the charges, the man raped his biological daughter, now 25, at several hotels in Tawau between December 2025 and March 2026.

items.title: Sabah Southern Madani Link to strengthen state’s electricity supply security, says Petra
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 22 — The Sabah Southern Madani Link (SSML) project by the Sabah state government is a strategic investment that strengthens the stability and security of the state’s electricity supply, according to the Ministry of Energy Transition and Water Transformation (Petra). The ministry said the project also supports the integration of new electricity supply generation, improves the cost efficiency of the electrical system, and strengthens Sabah’s position in the regional energy integration agenda. The ministry said the strategic SSML project was implemented to reinforce Sabah’s 275-kilovolt electricity transmission system and plays an important role in ensuring power from existing and new generation sources can be delivered, particularly to the state’s east coast.

items.title: Hiker dies at Bukit Maras peak, body carried down by rescuers
items.summary: KUALA NERUS, July 22 — A man was reported to have died at the summit of Bukit Maras here yesterday evening. Kuala Nerus Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) operations commander Muhammad Al Fadil Mat Amin said in the 6.48 pm incident, the victim, Nazli A. Hamid, 55, was found unconscious at the hilltop and was pronounced dead by a medical team that arrived at the scene. “Upon receiving the emergency call, six personnel from the Kuala Nerus Fire and Rescue Station (BBP) were deployed to the location.

items.title: MetMalaysia sounds thunderstorm warning for Kedah, Penang, Perak, Johor, Sabah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 22 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department has issued a thunderstorm warning for several states, forecasting heavy rain and strong winds across multiple regions this morning. The warning remains in effect until 11:00am today. The warning extends to Johor, specifically affecting Tangkak, Segamat, Muar, Batu Pahat, Pontian, and Johor Bahru.
